rationale: The citing paper uses Pauwels, Hanssens, and Siddarth (2002) as part of its core framing of what is known about long-term promotion effects. Beyond a generic list citation, it attributes specific prior findings to this study (e.g., only fleeting negative base-sales effects of promotions and promotion-related changes in price sensitivity) when developing expectations for discounting effects. This prior evidence helps motivate the citing paper's contribution of broadening analysis from promotion-heavy research to the full marketing mix.
relation_description: The citing paper treats the cited paper as prior empirical groundwork on long-term price-promotion dynamics. It builds its conceptual setup and hypothesis framing on those findings while extending the scope to include product and distribution effects across many categories.
